entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 1 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-01 23:12PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Heavy rain forecast for Sydney as bureau issues further warnings
  • Let him entertain you: Robbie Williams to headline 2022 AFL Grand Final
  • Harry addresses the 2022 Diana Awards ceremony with moving speech
  • Queensland man saves neighbour from burning house
  • The Sydney Morning Herald, Photos of the Week, June 30, 2022
  • Saints gun taken off after brutal head knock
  • Djokovic says there's still room for improvement
  • Anthony Albanese and French President Emmanuel Macron meet
  • National weather forecast for Saturday July 02
  • NSW communities prepare for multi-day deluge and flood warnings
  • Mask mandate could return to Queensland
  • Biden weighs up using federal sites for abortion care as legal challenges mount
  • Root-and-branch renewal as Australia makes migrant-majority history
  • ‘Diplomacy, diplomacy, diplomacy’: Ardern’s solution for preventing Chinese takeover of Taiwan
  • Australians get ‘truly extraordinary’ support from Sri Lankan fans
  • Russian missile hits Odesa after Ukraine retakes Snake Island
  • How a grand plan to spruik NSW became a Perrottet government nightmare
  • Perrottet’s plan to mobilise the Lismore ‘tinnie army’ for future catastrophes
  • Teals eye Guy’s seat, but Liberals back independents in Andrews’ backyard
  • Nikki clung to the gutter in rising floodwater for hours. A kayaker was her only hope for survival
  • ‘He’s not to blame’: Macron meets Albanese to forge fresh Franco-Australian ties
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Best of cartoons, July 2, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 1, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • ‘You’re chasing your tail’: Kelty warns against trying to outrun inflation with repeated wage rises
  • Strange bedfellows: After a rude shock, crossbenchers find common ground
  • ‘Oscars of architecture’ recognise the small and large, public and private
  • In the Herald: July 29, 1972
  • NSW rail dispute costing up to $45m a day as it veers towards all-out strike
  • Chinese developer arranged councillors’ travel, ICAC hears
  • Murderer put victim in unimaginable fear, court told
  • The Sydney church at odds with its conservative rivals
  • Severe weather warning for NSW ahead of weekend downpour
  • Guy Sebastian’s ‘painful chapter’ ends as ex-manager awaits sentence
  • ‘Wrong end of the shovel’: Experts say infrastructure cost-cutting is shrouded in secrecy
  • ‘This is war’: Inside the high-stakes world of schoolboy rowing
  • Titus Day leaves court awaiting sentence for embezzlement
  • Sydney commuters in for delayed, crowded and cancelled trains
  • Vision captures the moment a cruise ship hits an iceberg
  • Out-of-control bushfire inches closer to Machu Picchu
  • Meet John Lee, Hong Kong’s security enforcer turned new leader
  • Four people charged over smuggling tragedy in Texas
  • North Korea blames ‘alien things’ near border for COVID outbreak
  • Relentless rain in north-east India causes deadly mudslide
  • ‘Lessons learnt’ but all in the dark on bullying claims against Meghan
  • ‘You’re back’: Emmanuel Macron singles out Tanya Plibersek at oceans summit
  • ‘No excuses from me’: Marcos jnr vows to get it done like his father
  • Cruise ship damaged after it hits iceberg in Alaska
  • US to roll out Omicron booster shot, but it’s untested on humans
  • The Pink House at centre of America’s abortion wars
  • ‘They would kill me’: Ukraine’s Canberra envoy on his journey from Eurovision to Putin’s hit list
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Mass brawl on US cruise ship
  • Sinkhole swallows up minivan on US street
  • NSW Architecture Awards winners
  • Man arrested with shotgun in pants gets 15-year prison sentence
  • Expanded CityCat network could be a Games 2032 legacy
  • Townhouse bans and COVID booms: Brisbane’s missing housing strategy
  • Anthony Albanese hopes to reset relationship with France
  • Man allegedly attacked by strangers in Perth
  • Train strike to continue today
  • Health ministers meet to discuss the next wave of COVID-19
  • Sydney expected to see a month’s worth of rain in a day tomorrow
  • Guns, ink and short tempers: The police Viper versus the Nike bikies
  • Woman dies and boy critically injured in South Australian crash
  • Nuix and Macquarie’s dirty laundry aired in sizzling affidavit
  • The Wrap: ASX in the red after last-minute sell-off
  • What employees want from their jobs
  • Bitcoin’s big bet: Is crypto trading basically gambling?
  • The Australian brothers making history at the world’s most prestigious art school
  • Trailer: Where the Crawdads Sing
  • Trailer: The Forgiven
  • Trailer: Full Time
  • Trailer: The Black Phone
  • Trailer: Official Competition
  • Trailer: Hallelujah- Leonard Cohen, A Journey, A Song
  • Trailer: Lee Kernaghan- Boy From The Bush
  • Trailer: Falling for Figaro
  • Trailer: The Phantom of the Open
  • Trailer: The Gray Man
  • Pulitzer Prize winner’s new novel is a bold tale of triumph and tragedy
  • What to read: Jon Faine’s amazing case and a TV producer’s family secret
  • A serial killer strikes in this gripping Australian police procedural
  • Light festivals around Australia
  • The mysterious migration of eels unmasked by the Australian Museum
  • Amy Davidson has been hiding an entire tattoo sleeve from her father for years
  • Block out your weekend, Stranger Things is back with two huge new episodes
  • ‘Worst downturn in recent history’: Zuckerberg issues dire warning on economy
  • Schools struggling months after flood disaster
  • Bee-killer mite found on Central Coast as authorities look to destroy more hives
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • What We Learned This Term About the Supreme Court’s Shift to the Right
  • N.Y. State Senate Passes New Bill to Restrict Concealed Guns
  • New York Moves to Enshrine Abortion Rights in State Constitution
  • Abortion Abolitionists Want to Punish Women for Abortion
  • How Overturning Roe v Wade Will Impact Texas Families
  • The 20-Somethings Who Help the 70-Somethings Run Washington
  • Advanced U.S. Arms Make a Mark in Ukraine War, Officials Say
  • Brittney Griner, Women’s Basketball Star, Goes on Trial in Russia
  • With Climate Agenda Stalled at Home, Biden Still Hopes to Lead Abroad
  • What to Know About Flight Delays and Cancellations This Summer
  • Sun Valley Conference 2022: When Private Jets Land in Small-Town Idaho
  • Solveig Gold Is Proud to Be the Wife of a ‘Canceled’ Princeton Professor
  • The E.P.A. Ruling Goes Beyond Coal Regulation
  • America’s Post-Roe Chaos Is Here
  • On Guns, a Supreme Court Head-Scratcher: Is a Colonial Musket ‘Analogous’ to an AR-15?
  • Will Companies Help Their Employees Get Abortions?
  • Stop Asking Those Closest to Tragedy to Do the Heaviest Lifting
  • The Supreme Court Is the Final Word on Nothing
  • Why on Earth Is Pelosi Supporting the Trumpists?
  • The Supreme Court’s E.P.A. Decision Is More Gloom Than Doom
  • Your Friday Evening Briefing
  • The Real ‘Only Murders’ Building, and More: The Week in Narrated Articles
  • Women’s Euros Offer a Soccer Free-for-All
  • More Mexicans Are Migrating to the U.S. After a Decade-Long Drop
  • California Requires Plastics Makers to Foot the Bill for Recycling
  • Ukraine Live Updates: Missile Strikes Hit Near Odesa; Brittney Griner’s Trial in Russia Is Adjourned
  • ‘Mango Man’ Is the Fruit’s Foremost Poet, Philosopher, Fan and Scientist
  • ‘Long Live the Theater’: Mariupol’s Drama Company to Perform Again
  • Venice Will Require Tourists to Register and Pay Entry Fee
  • Tunisia’s President, Kais Saied, Proposes New Constitution
  • Why Is There So Much Turmoil in Eastern Congo?
  • What We Know About Brittney Griner’s Detention in Russia
  • Labeled a ‘Challenge’ by NATO, China Signals Its Own Hard-Line Worldview
  • Xi Jinping Tells a Muted Hong Kong That Political Power Is for Patriots
  • Denmark’s Leader Apologizes for Botched Pandemic Mink Cull
  • Yair Lapid, Israel’s New Prime Minister, Played the Long Game to Power
  • Why Does Israel Have So Many Elections?
  • Landslide in India Buries Dozens, Killing at Least 18
  • At Comedy Shows in Lviv, Crowds Look for Humor Amid a Deadly War
  • Biden Administration Offers an Offshore Drilling Plan. Likely Backers: Very Few.
  • Did the ‘Freedom Convoy’ Change the Meaning of Canada’s Flag?
  • From the Early Morning Sky, Shards of Hot Metal
  • Will There Be Enough Monkeypox Vaccine?
  • Benin Bronzes Returned to Nigeria from Germany
  • New Zealand Designates Proud Boys a Terrorist Group
  • Tropical Storm Bonnie Forms, Lashing Central America
  • Columbia Won’t Participate in the Next U.S. News Rankings
  • What’s happening in the states? Here’s the latest on which abortion laws are in effect and which are blocked.
  • The Perils of Slow Vote-Counting and Delayed Election Results
  • Jan. 6 Witness Anthony Ornato Is at the Center of a Battle Over Credibility
  • Vladimir Zelenko, 48, Dies; Promoted an Unfounded Covid Treatment
  • The Giant African Land Snail Has Been Spotted Again in Florida
  • Biden Will Award Medal of Freedom to Simone Biles, John McCain and Others
  • July 4 Weather Forecast: Expect a Hot, Humid Holiday Weekend
  • Black-Owned Bookstore Eso Won in South Los Angeles Is Closing
  • Russia Hints at Linking Griner’s Case to Fate of ‘Merchant of Death’
  • Rices Fire in California Burns More Than 900 Acres and Prompts Evacuations
  • Trump Group Pays for Jan. 6 Lawyers, Raising Concerns of Witness Pressure
  • E.P.A. Ruling Is Milestone in Long Pushback to Regulation of Business
  • Baltimore Banner, a News Start-Up, Aims to Challenge The Sun
  • Gas Prices Force Many to Rethink Driving, and Spending
  • Why Airport Employees Are Striking
  • Netflix Says It’s Business as Usual. Is That Good Enough?
  • Paying Down Credit Card Balances, Emergency Savings and Other Tips
  • Where Military Paychecks Are Prime Targets
  • Uber Safety Report Says Sexual Assaults Down but Rate of Traffic Deaths Up
  • Mark Zuckerberg Prepares Meta Employees for a Tougher 2022
  • Trump Media is subpoenaed in federal inquiry of Truth Social deal.
  • Pandemic-battered movie theaters are feeling good after a strong June.
  • Father of Technoblade, YouTube Minecraft Star, Says His Son Has Died
  • London Modern and Contemporary Auctions: A Market Minus the Froth
  • Fourth of July Weekend Will Test Airlines Struggling to Bounce Back
  • Auto Sear: $20 Device That Turns Handguns Into Automatic Weapons
  • How TikTok Became a Best Seller Machine
  • TikTok tells Republican senators how it plans to keep American data away from China.
  • GM Quarterly Sales Fall Amid Shortage in Computer Chips and Other Parts

News from the New York Times

entry.title: Washington Post: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Kyiv says at least 21 dead in strike near city of Odessa
  • Russia-Ukraine live updates: More than 20 dead in Odessa strikes, Kyiv says
  • U.S.-supplied HIMARS changing the calculus on Ukraine’s front lines
  • Ex-leader of Mexico’s search for disappeared convicted in DNA scandal
  • War in Ukraine may threaten a Syrian lifeline for aid
  • Who is Viktor Bout, Russian arms dealer eyed in rumored prisoner swap?
  • Tropical Storm Bonnie forms on path toward Nicaragua and Costa Rica
  • GOP officials back Hungary’s resistance to global tax deal, bucking Biden
  • Brittney Griner appears in Russian court over drug charges
  • Ukrainian borscht gets a spot on U.N. protection list
  • Resistance builds in Tunisia as populist leader seeks more power
  • Congo buries gold-crowned tooth, only remains of independence hero Lumumba
  • India imposes ban on single-use plastics. But will it be enforced?
  • China marks Hong Kong handover anniversary, as doubts hang over city
  • Biden pledges $800 million in new weapons for Ukraine, says U.S. gas prices will remain high
  • ‘Alien things’ brought covid into North Korea, regime says
  • China’s Xi ushers in the end of global Hong Kong
  • Russian missiles kill at least 21 in Ukraine's Odesa region
  • Daughter of slain Mexico reporter dies of wounds from attack
  • UN urges world action to cut 1.3 million road deaths in half
  • New Tropical Storm Bonnie heads for hit on Central America
  • $1.2M spent on search for Puerto Rican man found hiding
  • Protesters storm into parliament building in eastern Libya
  • Thousands protest migrant deaths at Spain-Morocco border
  • Brazil’s da Silva hints at 1-term presidency if elected
  • ‘Tiger King’ star charged with animal trafficking and money laundering
  • Debunking myths about vasectomies as their popularity increases post-Roe
  • Man arrested after taking ‘Star Wars’ R2-D2 droid at Disney, police say
  • Texas education board rejects proposal to call slavery ‘involuntary relocation’
  • After a Black man is killed by police, a city cancels its July Fourth celebration
  • A Pride Month marked by grief, rage and resilient joy
  • ‘Technoblade,’ a star Minecraft gamer on YouTube, dies of cancer at 23
  • Yellowstone visitor again gored by bison; second incident this week
  • Former ‘Cryptoqueen’ is now one of 10 most-wanted fugitives
  • She’s 2½. She took an IQ test and became Mensa’s youngest member.
  • He threatened to kill Joe Biden. His family says he’s a casualty of war.
  • Trauma physicians have become depressingly prepared for mass shootings
  • Justice Dept. subpoenas two Arizona state senators in Jan. 6 probe
  • Post Politics Now: Biden acknowledges there aren’t enough votes to change the filibuster on abortion
  • ‘Take me up to the Capitol now’: How close Trump came to joining rioters
  • Post Politics Now: Now a justice, Jackson eager to be ‘part of the promise of our great Nation’
  • How Trump World pressures witnesses to deny his possible wrongdoing
  • Remember RadioShack? It’s now a crypto company with wild tweets
  • Ellis Island immigrants weren’t special — today’s newcomers succeed just as quickly
  • Okay, Google: To protect women, collect less data about everyone
  • Google will delete user location history for abortion clinic visits
  • The best video games of 2022 so far
  • Want to play Wordle against your friends? Try Word Fight.
  • House adjacent to Congressional Country Club offered at $4.8M
  • Anxious About a Recession? Start Thinking Like a Freelancer
  • Joe Biden Has a Few Lessons for Gavin Newsom About Inflation
  • Companies Need to Get Real About Climate Risk
  • Biden’s Misguided Vow to Change the Filibuster for Abortion
  • What LNG Can and Can’t Do to Replace Europe’s Imports of Russian Gas
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • Why China Is Sticking With Its ‘Covid Zero’ Strategy
  • What Are the Policy Banks China Uses to Spur Economy?
  • As abortion rights collapse, Black and Brown women will suffer most
  • Brittney Griner is a hostage, plain and simple
  • An illustrated guide to this week’s surreal Jan. 6 committee hearing
  • Maybe the Democrats aren’t doomed in November
  • A firm’s split with its star gun-case lawyer shows what ails the left
  • Et tu, Alito? With murder of stare decisis, rule of law goes ad hoc
  • Ignore the progressives’ howling about the Supreme Court’s ‘legitimacy’
  • The voters speak in D.C., but it’s not quite clear what they said
  • In Dobbs, these untruths are self-evident
  • A ‘radical’ is coming for bank fees — and the industry hates it
  • The Jan. 6 committee’s zeal to impugn Trump is in danger of backfiring
  • A bold new push shows how blue states can resist the Supreme Court
  • The Supreme Court reminds the executive branch: Congress makes the laws
  • Prosecuting Trump could do more harm than good
  • The Supreme Court strikes again — this time at tribal sovereignty
  • It rubs the lotion on its skin
  • Cartoons by Ed Hall, Mike Luckovich and Michael Ramirez
  • The migrant tragedy in Texas is the result of a broken system
  • Flashbacks, anxiety and PTSD: Trauma is all around us in Ukraine
  • Everyone’s talking about the endgame in Ukraine. Here’s how it might look.
  • Vladimir Kara-Murza: Prison doesn’t give me many views of the sun
  • What the union vote at Towson’s Apple Store means for Maryland
  • Metro then and now
  • The D.C. area, with planning, can be a climate refuge
  • I spoke up against racism in Virginia and was attacked by ‘anti-racists’
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 1 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-01 23:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Beijing committed to ‘one country, two systems’ governing principle for Hong Kong, preserving city’s strengths, Xi Jinping says
  • Hong Kong should fill tech void in China, push for removal of curbs on start-ups in bid to become country’s international I&T hub, industry players urge
  • Hong Kong legal sector chiefs ‘encouraged’ by Xi Jinping’s remarks on preserving common law system and independent judiciary, but some lawyers express doubts
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee just got a to-do list from Xi Jinping. Now, he must take ‘concrete action’ to solve housing and help young people, analysts and residents say
  • Newly sworn-in Hong Kong leader John Lee vows to take a ‘can-do’ attitude to solve problems, day by day and ‘won’t let President Xi down’
  • 5 key takeaways from Xi’s Hong Kong speech, including Beijing’s ‘full jurisdiction’, no change to ‘one country, two systems’ and John Lee’s to-do list
  • ‘China’s bridge and window to the world’: here’s what President Xi Jinping had to say to Hongkongers in July 1 speech
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts advise against stricter social-distancing rules despite rise in cases with Omicron subvariants; city logs 2,318 infections
  • Hong Kong Observatory issues No 8 typhoon warning signal for first time in 2022 as Severe Tropical Storm Chaba nears city
  • John Lee must assure the world Hong Kong’s freedoms aren’t lost
  • Yoga instructor, 23, found dead with 30 stab and cut wounds in bathtub at 5-star Hong Kong hotel
  • US, UK and European officials call for China to honour Hong Kong commitments on handover anniversary
  • As it happened: Xi Jinping says Hong Kong’s unique status and advantages must be upheld as he hails ‘one country, two systems’
  • Hong Kong’s ‘new chapter’ under John Lee calls for reflecting on tumultuous past
  • Hong Kong’s former, incoming leaders attend July 1 flag-raising ceremony, Xi Jinping oversees swearing-in of John Lee
  • Xi Jinping in Hong Kong: what the Chinese president’s entourage tells us about Beijing’s thinking about the city
  • US user data is safe, TikTok CEO tells senators, amid fresh scrutiny over China tech giant’s access
  • Chinese short-video market in brutal shakeout as Tencent, Bilibili close smaller apps amid tougher conditions
  • Chinese firms in the US report revenue bounces back but investment drops with gloom about China-US ties
  • China hits out at West over comments on Hong Kong at 25th handover anniversary
  • Chinese game developer miHoYo, creator of Genshin Impact, sues Minmetals trust firm in a case of speculative investment gone bad
  • China’s Li Keqiang calls for new infrastructure push to shake off economic slowdown
  • Xi praises Hong Kong PLA garrison for city’s transition from ‘chaos’ to ‘stability’
  • Chinese envoy prods South Korea to rethink pro-US pivot
  • Two senior Tencent executives step down from vice-president roles to focus on jobs at literature and banking units: report
  • Chinese PLA’s biggest Y-20 warplanes boost aid delivery to quake-hit Afghanistan
  • Chinese scientists unlock mosquito mystery in scent study that could aid control of dengue and Zika
  • Coronavirus: China’s pandemic rule changes a nod to science on Omicron and economy, say experts
  • US hypersonic missile fails in test in latest setback for project
  • China fines another live-streaming influencer over tax evasion as Beijing keeps heat on once-booming industry
  • Chinese vice-president urges better South China Sea approach with Philippines as Marcos takes the reins
  • Asteroid’s ‘unique trajectory’ created world’s longest meteorite field in China
  • China’s factory activity expands at fastest pace in 13 months in June as demand returns
  • China’s tourists are trickling back after two years of coronavirus disruptions. But will it last?
  • Albanese, Macron cheer fresh start in Australia-France ties after submarine spat
  • Indonesia’s Jokowi met Putin and Zelensky to try to end war and reduce global food crisis. What did he achieve? Or was it Russian propaganda bonanza?
  • Singapore’s NEWBrew made from recycled toilet water ‘tastes just like beer’, fans say
  • Reliance empire succession: what to know about Akash Ambani, from his support of Mumbai Indians, Ivy League education to love of luxury cars
  • Singapore to hang another Malaysian drug trafficker next week
  • Thailand says Myanmar apologised for fighter jet airspace violation
  • Philippines’ Marcos Jnr appoints career diplomat Enrique Manalo as new foreign minister
  • India PM Narendra Modi’s meeting with UAE president Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan highlights New Delhi’s concerns about Gulf ties after Prophet Mohammed row
  • North Korea blames Covid-19 outbreak on ‘alien things’ near South Korea border
  • Ukraine accuses Russia of dropping phosphorus bombs on Snake Island after retreat
  • Jailed Paralympics star Oscar Pistorius meets murdered girlfriend’s father
  • Ukraine hails victory over Russia in ‘borscht war’ after Unesco ruling
  • Whose world? What order? Time has passed for the West to call the shots
  • WHO calls for urgent action as monkeypox cases triple in Europe
  • US basketball star Brittney Griner goes on trial over drug charges in Russia
  • Türkiye threatens to block Sweden-Finland Nato deal if promises not kept
  • UK in ‘summer of discontent’ as cost of living, worker anger soar
  • US Supreme Court limits government powers to curb greenhouse gases, upending Joe Biden’s climate agenda
  • Missile strikes kill 21 in Odesa after Ukraine retakes Snake Island
  • Blockchain with ‘Chinese characteristics’ quietly takes off with Beijing’s blessing while shunning cryptocurrency
  • Developing Asian countries shouldn’t have to choose between Western and Chinese investment – not when every dollar helps
  • Hong Kong developer Billion fails to sell a single flat at The Horizon project in Tai Po
  • Not too late to chase Chinese stocks ‘still near the bottom’ for Credit Suisse as others fret about zero-Covid pain
  • Meta slashes hiring plans for new engineers by 30 per cent as it girds for ‘fierce’ headwinds
  • US approves SpaceX’s Starlink satellite internet for use on planes and ships
  • China’s stocks drop on overheating signal, as index approaches bull-market territory after a five-week winning streak
  • Google to pay US$90 million to app developers to settle legal fight over app store service fees
  • Swire Properties, New World offer solutions for tenants to cut energy, water usage and reduce waste
  • Top Gun’s Tom Cruise at 60 – a look back at his life, from adopting Scientology to his marriages to Nicole Kidman and Katie Holmes and other defining moments
  • K-drama review: Kiss Sixth Sense – Disney+ fantasy romance starring Seo Ji-hye and Yoon Kye-sang ends on a barely satisfactory note
  • What is fawning? The people-pleasing trauma response that isn’t fight, flight or freeze – it’s about appeasing others to avoid conflict
  • Upcoming K-pop releases for summer 2022: BTS’ J-Hope, Itzy, Seventeen, Girls’ Generation and Ateez all dropping new content
  • 25 chefs who changed Hong Kong’s dining scene, from Michelin-starred restaurants to the man who promoted high-quality, affordable dim sum
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum: highlights to see among the national treasures on loan from Beijing, and its controversial beginnings
  • 7 rainy day activities in Hong Kong, from bowling to skating to visiting a library, that don’t involve shopping
  • In Netflix series The Gentlemen’s League 2, soccer amateurs from other sports in South Korea chase glory
  • Sustainable fashion in Japan and how 1 designer turned car airbags – near impossible to recycle – into clothes
  • Simu Liu on starting out poor in China, fights with his parents in Canada and his path to portraying Shang-Chi, Marvel Studios’ first Asian superhero
  • Who is Korean billionaire Thai Lee, the sixth-richest self-made woman in America? The Thai-born CEO of SHI International followed Buddhist wisdom to amass a US$4.1 billion fortune
  • 21 mirror-image female celebrities who could be twins: from Millie Bobby Brown’s resemblance to a young Natalie Portman, to Amy Adams and Isla Fisher, plus Penelope Cruz and Salma Hayek’s famous sisterly bond
  • Inside Rob Kardashian’s impressive net worth: he now famously avoids the spotlight, but Kim’s little brother still has millions to his name ... and a California mansion courtesy of mum Kris
  • Was Michael Jackson and Lisa Marie Presley’s marriage real? 19 years after Elvis’ only daughter saw The Jackson 5 perform, the pair eloped – but what did Donald Trump have to do with their romance?
  • Where to eat in Hong Kong in July: 5 new restaurants to try, from Crepes & Bakes inside the Palace Museum, to a Melrose Avenue-inspired coffee joint and the Ask For Alonzo team’s Porkcentric
  • 5 colourful new ‘it’ bags to buy for summer, from Loewe’s geometric Puzzle Hobo bag and Saint Laurent’s chic lambskin shoulder bag, to pieces by Balenciaga, Bottega Veneta and Valentino
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • US prepare for the unexpected ahead of World Cup qualifiers
  • Tsitsipas faces early hurdle in high-flying Kyrgios
  • Commentary: Why can't the fashion industry recycle discarded clothes?
  • Commentary: G7 and NATO summits lay bare hostile divide between China and the West

  • Sport-Restrictions on transgender athletes violate Olympic Charter - FIMS chief
  • Kerry vows US to meet climate goal despite court setback

  • Serie A appoint first female referee for next season
  • Germany hands first of Benin Bronzes back to Nigeria
  • Newcastle wrap up Botman signing from Lille
  • Crypto lender Voyager Digital suspends withdrawals, deposits
  • Palestinians pin scant hope on Biden visit after setbacks under Trump
  • LIV Golf plans 14 events, firmer teams in year two: report

  • Britain's Norrie reaches Wimbledon fourth round for first time
  • FTX signs deal with option to buy BlockFi for up to $240 million
  • Death toll from northeast India landslide rises to 20
  • Indonesia and United Arab Emirates reach trade pact
  • China urges US to fulfil climate duties after Supreme Court ruling
  • Japanese asked to save power as country's east sizzles
  • India bans many single-use plastics to tackle waste
  • Singaporean woman dies in car accident on Malaysia's East Coast Highway
  • North Korea blames 'alien things' near border with South Korea for COVID-19 outbreak
  • Reclusive Taliban leader attends gathering in Afghan capital
  • Singapore leaders congratulate China and Hong Kong on 25th handover anniversary
  • Russia seizes control of Sakhalin gas project, raises stakes with West
  • Hong Kong's 'true democracy' started after handover to China: President Xi Jinping
  • Freedom has 'vanished', Taiwan says on Hong Kong handover anniversary
  • Hong Kong's former security chief John Lee sworn in as city's new leader
  • Watch: Chinese President Xi Jinping speaks at the inauguration of new Hong Kong administration
  • China says New Zealand PM's comments on assertiveness 'wrong'
  • China's Great Wall Motor shelves $1 billion India plan -sources
  • Muslim pilgrims flock to Mecca for first post-pandemic haj
  • Boeing disappointed after China's top three airlines buy 300 Airbus planes
  • French police bust gang selling fake Bordeaux wine
  • Crypto exchange Coinbase looks to expand footprint in Europe
  • Top stablecoin Tether slashes commercial paper holdings amid crypto gloom
  • TikTok seeks to reassure lawmakers on US data security
  • Why sentiment in industrial metals has been crumbling
  • Ukraine war's disruption sends agriculture input prices up nearly 10% in Q1 - Eurostat
  • EU agrees on rulebook for 'Wild West' crypto markets
  • EMA issues second request for proposals to import low-carbon electricity into Singapore
  • China takes fresh steps to open bond market to foreigners
  • Indonesia tax amnesty finds $40 billion of unreported assets
  • Amazon to allow Prime users to unsubscribe in two clicks after EU complaints
  • Chip stocks fall as Micron outlook signals easing demand
  • Pakistan's June inflation surges to highest in over a decade
  • Banana cake from Malaysia recalled over preservative levels: SFA
  • Distribution of TraceTogether tokens scaled back amid falling demand

entry.title: Guardian: 1 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ireland manager Vera Pauw says she was raped and assaulted during playing days
  • Russia-Ukraine war: 19 dead after Odesa strike; Zelenskiy says EU membership should not take ‘years or decades’ – live

  • Moscow forcing teachers in occupied Ukraine to follow Russian curriculum

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
